world.title: Uruguayan national admits to scheme to circumvent US sanctions linked to Venezuela
world.description: A 60-year-old Uruguayan citizen pleaded guilty in a U.S. federal court to conspiring to operate an unlicensed money transmitting business in order to circumvent American sanctions linked to Venezuelan government officials, the Department of Justice announced on March 25.

world.description: Thailand has announced a major adjustment to its tourism strategy in response to declining long-haul arrivals, shifting greater focus toward domestic travel and short-haul markets amid global uncertainty and surging energy prices. - VnExpress International

science.title: Mechanisms behind tumor suppressor BAP1 highlight new treatment strategies for aggressive cancers
science.description: A team of scientists led by the National Cancer Center Singapore (NCCS) and Duke-NUS Medical School (Duke-NUS) has found a new approach for treating some of the world's most aggressive cancers associated with BAP1 mutations. Their research, published in the journal Science Translational Medicine on 1 April 2026, uncovers how BAP1 functions at the molecular level and presents a novel therapeutic strategy to slow the progression of mesothelioma, uveal melanoma, cholangiocarcinoma, and clear cell renal cell carcinoma harboring BAP1 mutations.

ai.title: AI disruption, inflation risks could tighten lending environment: QED’s Nigel Morris
ai.description: Nigel Morris, cofounder of QED Investors, expects lending companiess to tighten credit amid rising inflation and geopolitical tensions. While cautious in the short term, he remains positive on India’s fintech growth. However, according to CreditSights, a prolonged Middle East conflict could raise credit costs and tighten liquidity for lenders in India.

ai.title: Compulsory third language in Class 6, vocational education a must in Class 9-10: CBSE
ai.description: CBSE’s new curriculum, aligned with NEP 2020, mandates a third language for Class 6 students starting 2026-27. This paves the way for a third language to be included in the Class 10 board exam in 2031. The curriculum also includes mandatory subjects such as art education, vocational education, physical education, computational thinking, and artificial intelligence […]

ai.title: Korea's AI-Driven Healthcare Technologies Take Centre Stage at the upcoming WHX Bangkok 2026
ai.description: BANGKOK, April 3, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- WHX Bangkok 2026 organised by Informa Markets is set to be a landmark trade event for showcasing Korea's groundbreaking advancements in AI-driven healthcare and technology. Two leading Korean companies, Korea Body Information Co., Ltd. and maihub, will unveil their flagship solutions at the trade show taking place from 8 - 10 July 2026 at Queen Sirikit National Convention Centre (QSNCC) in Bangkok.

ai.title: The Persuasion Machine Inside AI How New Research Reveals A Quiet Trade
ai.description: INSIDE THIS REPORT A massive multi-institution study has uncovered something far more concerning than AI bias—it has identified how easily persuasion itself can be engineered. The most powerful influence tools are not hidden in advanced AI models, but in how they are trained and prompted. And the more persuasive these systems become, the less tethered A major multi-institution AI study reveals that conversational AI becomes more persuasive as it becomes less accurate, raising serious concerns about misinformation, political influence, and regulatory accountability.

ai.title: driven AI lets drones navigate without GPS signals or cameras
ai.description: AI-powered CLAK system lets drones navigate without GPS or cameras using onboard sensors like LiDAR and inertial data.
